title: Bellambi占퐏 Maid
content:
혻혻혻혻Amongst the thunder-splintered caves
혻혻혻혻On Ocean占퐏 long and windy shore,
혻혻혻혻I catch the voice of dying waves
혻혻혻혻Below the ridges old and hoar;
혻혻혻혻The spray descends in silver showers,
혻혻혻혻And lovely whispers come and go,
혻혻혻혻Like echoes from the happy hours
혻혻혻혻I never more may hope to know!
혻혻혻혻The low mimosa droops with locks
혻혻혻혻Of yellow hair, in dewy glade,
혻혻혻혻While far above the caverned rocks
혻혻혻혻I hear the dark Bellambi占퐏 Maid!

혻혻혻혻The moonlight dreams upon the sail
혻혻혻혻That drives the restless ship to sea;
혻혻혻혻The clouds troop past the mountain vale,
혻혻혻혻And sink like spirits down the lee;
혻혻혻혻The foggy peak of Corrimal,
혻혻혻혻Uplifted, bears the pallid glow
혻혻혻혻That streams from yonder airy hall
혻혻혻혻And robes the sleeping hills below;
혻혻혻혻The wandering meteors of the sky
혻혻혻혻Beneath the distant waters wade,
혻혻혻혻While mystic music hurries by
혻혻혻혻The songs of dark Bellambi占퐏 Maid!

혻혻혻혻Why comes your voice, you lonely One,
혻혻혻혻Along the wild harp占퐏 wailing strings?
혻혻혻혻Have not our hours of meeting gone,
혻혻혻혻Like fading dreams on phantom wings?
혻혻혻혻Are not the grasses round your grave
혻혻혻혻Yet springing green and fresh to view?
혻혻혻혻And does the gleam on Ocean占퐏 wave
혻혻혻혻Tide gladness now to me and you?
혻혻혻혻Oh! cold and cheerless falls the night
혻혻혻혻On withered hearts and hopes decayed:
혻혻혻혻And I have seen but little light
혻혻혻혻Since died the dark Bellambi占퐏 Maid!
meaning: About: Henry Clarence Kendall was an important nineteenth century Australian poet.
